mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) directly expands corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations adjust to higher fundamental cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity risk premiums and raises discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.50 percentage points within a single quarter
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline following quarterly FEDFUNDS hikes exceeding 0.50pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and declines in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which positively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day horizon specifically during periods of low inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not generate positive 5-day SPX returns when CPIAUCSL is above 3.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.2 million annualized rate
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when HOUST drops below 1.2 million, or the signal produces false positives in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real savings balances (RSAFS) combined with stable consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicate strong household purchasing power and reduced precautionary saving motives, which fuel equity demand and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising RSAFS and stable CPIAUCSL fails to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) erode real corporate margins and trigger monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX forward returns do not decline following consecutive monthly CPIAUCSL increases above 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 150,000 jobs signals an abrupt labor market contraction, reducing aggregate demand and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by >150,000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral following monthly PAYEMS declines greater than 150,000.
